title: CloudAudit Anomaly Rollup — Audit & Export Spec

quick_contract_index.diagnose_is_stateless: every explicit diagnose invocation writes pipeline_status=diagnosed and omits verified_summary/output_paths, regardless of existing repaired files or active workflow contents

issue_item_shape.required_term_case: case-sensitive for all three evidence strings, including agent-composed repair_action

quick_contract_index.read_in_full: This file is ~17KB; truncated reads miss required schema sections. Read it completely before implementing.

diagnose.state_contract: diagnose is stateless: always set pipeline_status to diagnosed and omit verified_summary/output_paths even when repair artifacts already exist or the active workflow is repaired

repair.output_dir_contract: must write all repair outputs under the provided --output-dir path; do not hardcode /app/output when a custom path is supplied

evidence.pipeline_evidence_format: literal substring excerpt from frozen original workflow source
evidence.term_matching: case-sensitive substring match for every required_terms_by_issue entry in dossier_quote, pipeline_evidence, and repair_action; repair_action is agent-composed but its required terms still match case-sensitively

input_stats.exact_key_names: input_stats keys must be exactly alert_count, unique_alert_ids, asset_groups

output_paths.key_naming_contract: keys are semantic snake_case names, not filenames

removed_tokens_schema.description: Map each forbidden_executable_tokens literal string to a boolean that indicates it was removed from executable behavior.
removed_tokens_schema.required_key_source: keys must be the exact literal strings from forbidden_executable_tokens (no semantic aliases)

pre_repair.capture_rule: pipeline_source_sha256 is hashlib.sha256(Path('/app/workflow/.export_report.original').read_bytes()).hexdigest(); pipeline_tokens_present is computed from that frozen original before applying any patch

pipeline_tokens_present_schema.description: Map each forbidden_executable_tokens literal string to a boolean that indicates it was present before repair.
pipeline_tokens_present_schema.required_key_source: keys must be the exact literal strings from forbidden_executable_tokens (no semantic aliases)

workflow_repair.implementation_freedom: helper names, constant names, normalization syntax, graph representation, and sorting syntax are implementation details; generated behavior is contractual
workflow_repair.acceptance_contract: The repaired workflow must satisfy canonicalization and outputs without relying on test or solution artifacts.

repair_state_contract.self_contained: every log_audit.py repair invocation must write a correct repaired implementation to /app/workflow/export_report.py before running it
repair_state_contract.starting_state: verification may copy /app/workflow/.export_report.original over the active workflow immediately before repair, including with a custom --output-dir; never assume solve.sh or an earlier command already patched it
sequence: capture pre-repair hash and token evidence from the frozen original
run it with the requested output directory
run it again to verify idempotency

summary_json.override_excluded_count: count of anomaly candidates excluded by canonicalized override windows
summary_json.override_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of the exact payload defined by override_checksum_serialization

override_checksum_serialization.row_order: sort by (asset_group,severity_scope) ascending, then compacted interval (start_ms,end_ms) ascending
override_checksum_serialization.trailing_delimiter: none; do not append LF or | after the final row

summary_json.max_override_pressure_score: maximum override_pressure_score across final anomaly rows (0 when none)
summary_json.chain_count: number of distinct chain_id values across final anomaly rows
summary_json.max_chain_risk_score: maximum chain_risk_score across final anomaly rows (0 when none)
summary_json.chain_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(chain_digest values in final anomaly order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.max_chain_reach_score: maximum chain_reach_score across final anomaly rows (0 when none)
summary_json.chain_reach_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(chain_reach_digest values in final anomaly order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.anomaly_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(anomaly_digest values in final anomaly order)) lowercase 64 hex chars

service_matrix_json.description: Flat map of asset_group -> per-severity counts from canonical deduped alerts.

flagged_jsonl.sort: observed_ms desc, severity rank desc, chain_risk_score desc, chain_reach_score desc, override_pressure_score desc, alert_id asc

field_types.chain_reach_path: JSON array (list) of chain_id strings naming the strongest reach path in order. Emit the array itself, not a joined string. The 'comma_joined_chain_reach_path' token used inside chain_reach_digest and anomaly_digest is a DIGEST-ONLY derivation (','.join of this array) and does not change the emitted field type, which is the array.

guardrails.test_artifacts: repaired source must not reference /tests, expected_summary.json, alt_events.json, or /solution, and runtime reads from /tests are forbidden
guardrails.generalization: derive outputs from --input and fixed operational data sources rather than fixture values

digest_payloads.chain_digest: first 12 lowercase SHA256 hex chars of chain_id|chain_size|chain_span_ms|chain_risk_score|comma_joined_chain_alert_ids
digest_payloads.chain_id: first 10 lowercase SHA1 hex chars of comma_joined_chain_alert_ids
digest_payloads.chain_reach_digest: first 12 lowercase SHA256 hex chars of chain_id|chain_reach_score|chain_reach_depth|comma_joined_chain_reach_path
digest_payloads.anomaly_digest: the only authoritative definition: first 12 lowercase SHA1 hex chars of alert_id|observed_ms|severity|asset_group|signature|override_pressure_score|chain_id|chain_size|chain_span_ms|chain_risk_score|chain_digest|chain_reach_score|chain_reach_depth|comma_joined_chain_reach_path|chain_reach_digest
digest_payloads.escalation_ledger_checksum: SHA-256 over one line per anomaly row in flagged.jsonl export order, `alert_id|escalation_pressure|c|carry_out` with c=1 for an escalation-critical row else 0, joined by a single newline, no trailing newline, hashed over UTF-8. The values themselves are governed by the dossier.

semantics_source.notes: This document is the output contract only: CLI shapes, report schemas, exact key sets, checksum serialization and digest payload encodings. It does NOT define how values are derived.
semantics_source.authority: Normalization, dedupe tie-breaks, override windows/matching/pressure, chain correlation and the directed reach propagation were settled during the CloudAudit review; the authoritative record is the #CloudAudit-ticketed decision notes inside /app/incident/export_dossier.md. Where an early triage proposal conflicts with a later decision, the later decision wins.

critical_escalation_ids.note: alert_id values as strings, sorted -- not in export order.

field_types._note: Declares the exact key name, JSON type and container shape of every field the verifier matches literally. These are output-format facts only; how each value is derived is governed by the dossier's ticketed decisions.

pipeline_status.note: The key is named exactly `pipeline_status`. `diagnosis`, `status` and `state` are not accepted. A diagnose run reports `diagnosed`; a repair run reports `repaired`.

input_stats.note: Exactly these three keys, no aliases and no extras.

asset_groups.note: A JSON array of the distinct asset-group names, sorted. It is NOT a count -- returning an integer here fails, even when the integer equals the array length.

alert_count.note: Number of alert records read from the input, counted BEFORE deduplication.

unique_alert_ids.note: Number of distinct alert_id values in the input. This is the deduplicated count; alert_count is not.

raw_alert_count.note: verified_summary field. Raw input record count BEFORE deduplication -- do not substitute the deduplicated count here.

issues_found_item.note: Every finding carries all five keys; `evidence` is itself an object with exactly `dossier_quote`, `pipeline_evidence` and `repair_action`. Omitting severity, description or resolution fails the schema check.
